title: Fire-drill evacuation metrics never complete when camera is outside the factory
detail: startEmergencyDrill(workerCount) snapshots totalWorkers from useProductionStore workers.length (always 12). EmergencyControlPanel.tsx:75 and SafetyPanel.tsx:74 both call `startEmergencyDrill(workerCount)`. But markWorkerEvacuated only fires from a mounted Worker component, and the entire WorkerSystemNew is gated in MillScene.tsx:824 by `showInterior` (= isCameraInside || isCameraInDockZone, MillScene.tsx:739). gameSimulationStore.ts:862: `const evacuationComplete = newEvacuatedIds.length >= state.drillMetrics.totalWorkers;`. If the user orbits/zooms out to watch the evacuation (the natural action), the Worker components unmount (manager.register cleanup deregisters them), so evacuatedWorkerIds never reaches 12: evacuationComplete stays false, finalTimeSeconds stays null, the progress bar sticks and the 'ALL CLEAR' banner never appears. Manual END DRILL still works (endEmergencyDrill stops the alarm), so the alarm is not stuck, but the drill never self-completes.
fix: Product decision among: (a) cap totalWorkers to the count of currently-registered/mounted workers at drill start; (b) drive evacuation headlessly from the store so it completes regardless of camera; or (c) keep WorkerSystemNew (or a headless evacuation tick) mounted while a drill is active. Do not pick blindly; this changes drill UX/semantics.
_area: L1 Correctness · Entry + machines + workers + logistics

title: Physics forklift registers isStopped=false while halted by standalone emergency stop
detail: Physics is default-ON (graphicsStore.ts:122 `enablePhysics: true`), so PhysicsForklift is the live path. Line 131: `const isStopped = emergencyDrillMode || operationRef.current !== 'traveling';` — it omits forkliftEmergencyStop. When the standalone E-stop is engaged (EmergencyStopButton.tsx toggles setForkliftEmergencyStop), the forklift is physically halted at line 152-154 (`if (emergencyDrillMode || forkliftEmergencyStop) { rb.setLinvel({x:0,y:0,z:0}); ... }`), but it then registers isStopped=false into positionRegistry. Workers querying the registry (isForkliftApproaching / nearest-forklift stopped state) treat the halted forklift as moving and may needlessly evade a stationary forklift. The code comment at line 129-130 even states isStopped 'should include emergency drill mode AND loading/unloading' but forgets the standalone E-stop.
fix: Read forkliftEmergencyStop in updatePosition and include it: `const isStopped = emergencyDrillMode || forkliftEmergencyStop || operationRef.current !== 'traveling';` (forkliftEmergencyStop is already subscribed at line 97). Add it to the updatePosition useCallback deps as well.
_area: L1 Correctness · Entry + machines + workers + logistics

title: Legacy forklift wheel refs go stale after a close→far→close LOD cycle (wheels stop spinning)
detail: Legacy (non-physics) path only. Lines 874-876: `if (wheelRefsRef.current.length === 0 && ref.current.children.length >= 12) { wheelRefsRef.current = ref.current.children.slice(8, 12) as THREE.Mesh[]; }`. The capture guard only fires while the array is empty. On a close→far→close LOD round-trip, ForkliftModel unmounts (replaced by ForkliftBillboard at distanceTier 'far') and remounts with NEW child meshes, but wheelRefsRef still holds the old (now-detached) meshes and the `length === 0` guard never re-fires, so the loop at 877-879 mutates detached meshes and the visible wheels stop animating. Visual-only and legacy-only.
fix: Reset wheelRefsRef.current to [] when distanceTier transitions back to 'close' (e.g. in a useEffect on distanceTier), or re-capture when the cached mesh is no longer parented to ref.current. Visual change — verify in-app.
_area: L1 Correctness · Entry + machines + workers + logistics

title: Unguarded pathActions index access can throw if path/pathActions lengths diverge
detail: Line 839: `const action = data.pathActions[pathIndexRef.current];` followed by `action.type` reads at 842/849. pathIndexRef is bounded by `% data.path.length` (line 858), NOT by pathActions.length. The two hard-coded forklifts currently have matching lengths (8/8 and 6/6) so there is no live crash, but if path and pathActions ever diverge (e.g. a new route edited in one array only), action is undefined and `action.type` throws inside useFrame, killing the render loop. Defensive gap, not a live failure.
fix: Guard: `const action = data.pathActions[pathIndexRef.current]; if (!action) { pathIndexRef.current = (pathIndexRef.current + 1) % data.path.length; currentTarget.current.set(...data.path[pathIndexRef.current]); return; }` — or assert path.length === pathActions.length at construction.
_area: L1 Correctness · Entry + machines + workers + logistics

title: Machine panel re-registers under a fresh random id on every status change
detail: Lines 41-55: the registration effect depends on [enabled, status] and computes `const id = `panel-${Math.random()}`` inside the effect body. On each status transition (running→warning→critical, which is routine), the effect tears down (unregisterPanel(prevId)) and re-registers under a brand-new random id, churning panelRegistry entries. Registration is also conditional (`if (screenMaterial.current)`) while cleanup is unconditional, so an unregister can run for an id that was never registered (harmless no-op). No leak because each effect run closes over its own id, but the random-id-per-status pattern is fragile and makes the registry key non-deterministic.
fix: Generate a stable id once with useId() or a useRef, e.g. `const idRef = useRef(`panel-${useId()}`)`, and use idRef.current in both register and unregister so the same panel keeps one identity across status changes.
_area: L1 Correctness · Entry + machines + workers + logistics
